mcqs.question: Which constructor of JButton creates a button with no text or icon?
mcqs.explanation: JButton() is the no-argument constructor that creates a button with no text or icon. The other constructors require either a string, an Icon, or an Action object as parameter.

mcqs.question: What interface must a class implement to handle JButton click events?
mcqs.explanation: ActionListener is the standard interface for handling button click events. Its actionPerformed() method is called when the button is clicked. MouseListener can also detect clicks but is not the preferred way for buttons.

mcqs.question: Which method is used to set the text displayed on a JButton?
mcqs.explanation: The setText(String text) method is used to set or change the button's displayed text. setLabel() is used in AWT Button class, not in Swing JButton.

mcqs.question: What happens when you click a JButton that has been disabled using setEnabled(false)?

options.text: The button appears normal but doesn't respond

mcqs.explanation: When a JButton is disabled, it cannot generate action events. The button appears grayed out and does not respond to user interactions. This is useful for implementing conditional button availability.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid JButton constructor?
mcqs.explanation: JButton does not have a constructor that takes an int as parameter. The valid constructors are: JButton(), JButton(String text), JButton(Icon icon), and JButton(String text, Icon icon).

mcqs.question: What is the default behavior of the Enter key when a JButton has focus?
mcqs.explanation: When a JButton has focus, pressing the Enter key triggers the button's action, similar to clicking it with the mouse. This is part of Swing's accessibility features and keyboard navigation support.

mcqs.question: How can you associate a specific string command with a JButton for event handling?
mcqs.explanation: setActionCommand(String cmd) sets the action command string for the button. This string is sent to ActionListeners via the ActionEvent object, allowing you to identify which button was clicked.

mcqs.question: In the MVC architecture used by Swing, what role does JButton play?
mcqs.explanation: JButton acts as both View and Controller in MVC. It provides the visual representation (View) and handles user input to trigger actions (Controller). The underlying data (Model) is typically simple for buttons.

mcqs.question: What method is used to set a keyboard mnemonic (shortcut) for a JButton?
mcqs.explanation: setMnemonic(char key) sets the keyboard mnemonic for the button, allowing users to activate it using Alt+the specified character. This enhances accessibility and keyboard navigation.

mcqs.question: Which Swing package contains the JButton class?
mcqs.explanation: JButton is part of the javax.swing package. The java.awt package contains the older AWT Button class, not JButton. javax.swing provides all lightweight Swing components.

mcqs.question: What is the return type of JButton's getActionCommand() method?
mcqs.explanation: getActionCommand() returns a String representing the action command associated with the button. If no action command has been set, it returns the button's text by default.

mcqs.question: When a JButton is clicked, which method of ActionListener is invoked?
mcqs.explanation: The actionPerformed(ActionEvent e) method is the only method in the ActionListener interface. It is called when the button is clicked. This method must be implemented by any class that handles button events.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of using AbstractAction with JButton?

options.text: To separate action definition from listener implementation
To add multiple listeners at once

mcqs.explanation: AbstractAction provides a reusable implementation of ActionListener. You can define the actionPerformed() logic in an AbstractAction and attach it to multiple buttons, separating the action definition from the event-handling code.

mcqs.question: Which method would you use to change the cursor displayed when hovering over a JButton?
mcqs.explanation: setCursor(Cursor c) is inherited from JComponent and can be used to change the cursor displayed when hovering over the button. For example, you can set it to Cursor.HAND_CURSOR for a clickable appearance.

mcqs.question: What happens if you add the same ActionListener to a JButton multiple times?

options.text: The listener is added only once
The listener is called multiple times per click

mcqs.explanation: If you add the same ActionListener instance multiple times, its actionPerformed() method will be called multiple times (once for each registration) when the button is clicked. This is a common bug to avoid.

mcqs.question: Which method of JComponent allows you to register for rollover events on a JButton?
mcqs.explanation: Mouse motion events like mouseEntered and mouseExited can be tracked using addMouseMotionListener(). However, Swing also provides a built-in rollover effect mechanism through the LookAndFeel that doesn't require explicit listeners.

mcqs.question: In the ButtonModel interface, which method returns whether the button is currently armed (ready to be pressed)?
mcqs.explanation: The isArmed() method of ButtonModel returns true when the button is armed - meaning the mouse has been pressed on the button but not yet released. This state is part of the button's internal model in the MVC architecture.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the setRolloverEnabled(boolean) method in JButton?

options.text: To make the button appear only on mouse hover
To enable visual feedback when mouse hovers over the button
To automatically hide the button after hover

mcqs.explanation: setRolloverEnabled(boolean) enables or disables the rollover effect - visual changes that occur when the mouse cursor is over the button. When true (default in many LookAndFeels), the button shows special highlighting during mouse rollover.
